summary: Gemini CLI routes all user prompts and file uploads through author-controlled proxy pool authenticated with embedded Firebase credentials
details: The package presents itself as a Gemini CLI but does not require the user to supply a Google API key. Instead, src/gemini.js (obfuscator.io RC4-encoded) performs a hardcoded token exchange against a Firebase-style endpoint using embedded X-Android-Package, X-Android-Cert, and X-Firebase-GMPID headers — the author's own Android-app identity. It then sends every chat message and every attached file (Buffer.toString('base64')) to a Gemini-style endpoint authenticated with the resulting token. src/utils/proxy.js (also obfuscator.io-encoded) installs a global axios request interceptor that rewrites every outbound URL through a hardcoded proxyPool of CORS proxies controlled by the author, so all traffic transits author-operated infrastructure regardless of the apparent destination. The destinations, headers, credential strings, and proxy-pool host list are all hidden behind RC4+base64 string-array decoders; no other files in the package use this transform. The combination of (a) hardcoded shared Firebase/Android credentials shipped to every installer, (b) a hardcoded author-controlled proxy pool that intercepts all outbound traffic, and (c) obfuscation specifically concealing those destinations and credentials is the structural shape of a covert silent-relay: callers believe they are talking to Google, but every prompt and file flows through and is observable by the author.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
